chunks:
# qualify_lead_intent.yaml
# Task-Level Intent Example - Lead Qualification Function
# This shows how to document a single function/task with IVD

# Reference parent module intent (this task is part of lead_qualifier module)
parent_intent: "../lead_qualifier_intent.yaml"

# SCOPE: Task-level intent
scope:
  level: "task"
  type: "function"
  granularity: "fine"

# METADATA: Optional - for feature inventory (large projects)
metadata:
  feature_id: "qualify_lead"
  category: "sales"
  tags: ["leads", "qualification", "threshold"]
  status: "implemented"

# WHAT should this do?
intent:
  summary: "Qualify a single lead based on score and data quality thresholds"
  goal: |
    Determine if a lead meets qualification criteria for sales outreach.
    This is a critical gating function that filters the sales pipeline,
    balancing quality (don't waste sales time) vs quantity (maintain
    sufficient pipeline for revenue goals).
  success_metric: "85% precision, 70% recall on historical test set"
  stakeholders:
    - "VP Sales (Jane Smith)"
    - "Sales Operations Team"
    - "Lead Scoring System"

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# INTERFACE: Crystal clear inputs and outputs
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------

interface:
  signature: "qualify_lead(lead: Lead, config: QualificationConfig) -> QualificationResult"
  
  inputs:
    - name: "lead"
      type: "Lead"
      description: "Lead object containing score and data quality metrics"
      required: true
      constraints:
        - "lead.score must be float between 0.0 and 1.0"
        - "lead.data_quality must be float between 0.0 and 1.0"
        - "lead.id must be non-empty string"
      example:
        id: "lead_12345"
        score: 0.75
        data_quality: 0.85
        company: "Acme Corp"
        contact_email: "john@acme.com"
      validation: "Pydantic Lead model validates structure and ranges"
      
    - name: "config"
      type: "QualificationConfig"
      description: "Configuration with qualification thresholds"
      required: false
      default: "QualificationConfig.default()"
      constraints:
        - "score_threshold between 0.0 and 1.0"
        - "data_quality_threshold between 0.0 and 1.0"
      example:
        score_threshold: 0.70
        data_quality_threshold: 0.80
      validation: "Pydantic QualificationConfig validates thresholds"
  
  outputs:
    - name: "result"
      type: "QualificationResult"
      description: "Qualification decision with reasoning"
      structure: |
        QualificationResult(
            qualified: bool,
            score: float,
            data_quality: float,
            reason: str,
            metadata: dict
        )
      constraints:
        - "qualified is always boolean (never None)"
        - "score and data_quality match input values"
        - "reason explains why qualified or not qualified"
        - "metadata contains timestamp and threshold values used"
      example:
        qualified: true
        score: 0.75
        data_quality: 0.85
        reason: "Lead meets both score (0.75 >= 0.70) and data quality (0.85 >= 0.80) thres

th reasoning"
      structure: |
        QualificationResult(
            qualified: bool,
            score: float,
            data_quality: float,
            reason: str,
            metadata: dict
        )
      constraints:
        - "qualified is always boolean (never None)"
        - "score and data_quality match input values"
        - "reason explains why qualified or not qualified"
        - "metadata contains timestamp and threshold values used"
      example:
        qualified: true
        score: 0.75
        data_quality: 0.85
        reason: "Lead meets both score (0.75 >= 0.70) and data quality (0.85 >= 0.80) thresholds"
        metadata:
          timestamp: "2026-01-23T10:30:00Z"
          score_threshold: 0.70
          data_quality_threshold: 0.80
          version: "3.0"
      
    - name: "side_effects"
      type: "effects"
      description: "Observable effects of calling this function"
      effects:
        - "Logs qualification decision to CloudWatch"
        - "Increments qualification metrics counter (Prometheus)"
        - "NO database writes (function is read-only)"
        - "NO external API calls (pure computation)"
  
  exceptions:
    - exception: "ValidationError"
      when: "Lead object fails validation (invalid score/data_quality range)"
      handling: "Raise immediately with detailed validation errors"
      example: "ValidationError: lead.score must be between 0.0 and 1.0, got 1.5"
      
    - exception: "TypeError"
      when: "Input types are incorrect (not Lead or QualificationConfig)"
      handling: "Raise immediately with type error message"
      example: "TypeError: Expected Lead, got dict"
  
  dependencies:
    - dependency: "logger"
      type: "logging"
      required: true
      fallback: "None (function fails if logger not available)"
      description: "Logs all qualification decisions for audit trail"
      
    - dependency: "metrics"
      type: "monitoring"
      required: false
      fallback: "Skip metrics if not available (function still works)"
      description: "Records qualification metrics for monitoring"

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# BEHAVIOR: How this function behaves
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------

behavior:
  preconditions:
    - "lead object is valid (passes Pydantic validation)"
    - "config thresholds are in valid range [0.0, 1.0]"
    - "logger is initialized and available"
  
  postconditions:
    - "QualificationResult is always returned (never None)"
    - "qualified field is always boolean"
    - "reason field is always non-empty string"
    - "Function never modifies input objects (pure function)"
    - "Execution is logged (audit trail exists)"
  
  invariants:
    - "Function is pure (no side effects except logging)"
    - "Function is deterministic (same input = same output)"
    - "Function is idempotent (can call multiple times safely)"
    - "Function is thread-safe (no shared mutable state)"
    - "Function never raises exceptions for valid inputs"
  
  performance:
    - metric: "latency"
      target: "p50 < 5ms, p95 < 10ms, p99 < 20ms"
      critical

ed (never None)"
    - "qualified field is always boolean"
    - "reason field is always non-empty string"
    - "Function never modifies input objects (pure function)"
    - "Execution is logged (audit trail exists)"
  
  invariants:
    - "Function is pure (no side effects except logging)"
    - "Function is deterministic (same input = same output)"
    - "Function is idempotent (can call multiple times safely)"
    - "Function is thread-safe (no shared mutable state)"
    - "Function never raises exceptions for valid inputs"
  
  performance:
    - metric: "latency"
      target: "p50 < 5ms, p95 < 10ms, p99 < 20ms"
      critical_threshold: "p95 > 50ms"
      measurement: "tests/test_performance.py::test_qualify_lead_latency"
      
    - metric: "memory"
      target: "< 1MB per call"
      critical_threshold: "> 10MB per call"
      measurement: "memory_profiler on test suite"
      
    - metric: "cpu"
      target: "< 10ms CPU time"
      critical_threshold: "> 100ms CPU time"
      measurement: "cProfile on test suite"

# WHAT must hold true?
constraints:
  - name: "precision_minimum"
    requirement: "precision >= 0.85"
    test: "tests/test_qualification.py::test_precision_threshold"
    consequence_if_violated: "Sales team wastes time on unqualified leads, conversion rate drops"
    current_value: 0.87
    measurement: "Measured on 500-lead historical test set"
    
  - name: "recall_minimum"
    requirement: "recall >= 0.70"
    test: "tests/test_qualification.py::test_recall_threshold"
    consequence_if_violated: "Insufficient qualified leads, pipeline too small for revenue goals"
    current_value: 0.72
    measurement: "Measured on 500-lead historical test set"
    
  - name: "type_safety"
    requirement: "Function raises TypeError for invalid input types"
    test: "tests/test_qualification.py::test_type_validation"
    consequence_if_violated: "Runtime errors in production, crashes downstream systems"
    
  - name: "determinism"
    requirement: "Same input produces same output (deterministic)"
    test: "tests/test_qualification.py::test_deterministic"
    consequence_if_violated: "Inconsistent behavior confuses sales team, breaks reproducibility"

# WHY this approach?
rationale:
  decision: |
    Use simple threshold-based qualification (score >= 0.70 AND data_quality >= 0.80)
    
    Key decisions:
    1. Pure function (no side effects except logging)
    2. Explicit thresholds (not ML-based)
    3. Both conditions must pass (AND, not OR)
    4. Return structured result (not just boolean)
  
  reasoning: |
    WHY pure function?
    - Easier to test (no mocking needed)
    - Easier to reason about (no hidden state)
    - Thread-safe by default
    - Can be cached/memoized easily
    
    WHY explicit thresholds not ML?
    - Thresholds are business decisions (needs stakeholder approval)
    - ML models can drift over time
    - Thresholds are easier to explain to sales team
    - Previous ML classifier (v2) overfitted on training data
    
    WHY both conditions (AND)?
    - High score with poor data = can't follow up (missing contact info)
    - Good data with low score = likely to waste sales time
    - Both must pa

reasoning: |
    WHY pure function?
    - Easier to test (no mocking needed)
    - Easier to reason about (no hidden state)
    - Thread-safe by default
    - Can be cached/memoized easily
    
    WHY explicit thresholds not ML?
    - Thresholds are business decisions (needs stakeholder approval)
    - ML models can drift over time
    - Thresholds are easier to explain to sales team
    - Previous ML classifier (v2) overfitted on training data
    
    WHY both conditions (AND)?
    - High score with poor data = can't follow up (missing contact info)
    - Good data with low score = likely to waste sales time
    - Both must pass for meaningful sales outreach
    
    WHY structured result not boolean?
    - Downstream systems need reasoning (why qualified/not)
    - Metadata enables debugging (what thresholds were used?)
    - Audit trail requires full context (not just True/False)
  
  evidence:
    - path: "playground/lead_qualification_analysis_2025-12.ipynb"
      description: "Threshold analysis on 500 historical leads"
      key_findings: "0.70/0.80 thresholds achieved 85% precision, 70% recall"
      
    - path: "docs/decisions/ADR-089-lead-qualification-thresholds.md"
      description: "Architecture Decision Record for threshold selection"
      
    - path: "tests/test_qualification.py"
      description: "100% test coverage, all edge cases validated"
  
  date: "2025-12-15"
  stakeholder: "VP Sales (Jane Smith)"
  approval_evidence: "Email approval 2025-12-15, Slack #sales-ops channel"

# WHY NOT alternatives?
alternatives:
  - name: "ML-based classifier (Random Forest)"
    description: "Train ML model on historical lead conversion data"
    rejected_because: |
      Attempted in v2 (2025-12-01). Model achieved 92% accuracy in training
      but only 78% in production due to overfitting. Sales team feedback
      negative: "Too many false positives, model behavior unpredictable."
    experiment: "playground/ml_classifier_eval_2025-12.ipynb"
    date_tested: "2025-12-01"
    outcome: "Deprecated after 2 weeks due to poor production performance"
    
  - name: "Rule-based classification (complex rules)"
    description: "Multiple if/else rules based on industry, company size, location, etc."
    rejected_because: |
      Too brittle. Required constant maintenance as business rules changed.
      Rule conflicts made behavior unpredictable. Team spent more time
      debugging rules than improving lead generation.
    experiment: "v1 implementation (deprecated 2025-11-15)"
    date_tested: "2025-10-01 to 2025-11-15"
    outcome: "Replaced with threshold-based approach"
    
  - name: "Single threshold (score only, ignore data quality)"
    description: "Qualify based only on score >= 0.70"
    rejected_because: |
      Incident #456: High-score leads with missing contact info created
      sales frustration. 40% of qualified leads couldn't be contacted.
      Data quality gate (0.80) reduced this to < 5%.
    experiment: "A/B test 2025-11-20 to 2025-11-30"
    date_tested: "2025-11-20"
    outcome: "Data quality gate added, incident #456 resolved"
    
  - name: "OR logic (score OR data_quality)"
    description: "Qualify if EI

1 to 2025-11-15"
    outcome: "Replaced with threshold-based approach"
    
  - name: "Single threshold (score only, ignore data quality)"
    description: "Qualify based only on score >= 0.70"
    rejected_because: |
      Incident #456: High-score leads with missing contact info created
      sales frustration. 40% of qualified leads couldn't be contacted.
      Data quality gate (0.80) reduced this to < 5%.
    experiment: "A/B test 2025-11-20 to 2025-11-30"
    date_tested: "2025-11-20"
    outcome: "Data quality gate added, incident #456 resolved"
    
  - name: "OR logic (score OR data_quality)"
    description: "Qualify if EITHER threshold is met"
    rejected_because: |
      Increased recall to 85% but dropped precision to 65%. Sales team
      overwhelmed with low-quality leads. 15% more leads but 20% lower
      conversion rate = net negative.
    experiment: "playground/threshold_logic_comparison_2025-12.ipynb"
    date_tested: "2025-12-05"
    outcome: "AND logic is correct (both must pass)"

# WHAT could go wrong?
risks:
  - condition: "Precision drops below 0.80 (currently 0.87)"
    impact: "high"
    probability: "medium"
    action: "Alert VP Sales, review threshold increase to 0.75"
    monitor: "CloudWatch dashboard: lead_qualification_precision metric"
    threshold: "Alert if precision < 0.80 for 7 consecutive days"
    severity: "high"
    mitigation: "Weekly precision review in sales ops meeting"
    
  - condition: "Recall drops below 0.60 (currently 0.72)"
    impact: "medium"
    probability: "low"
    action: "Alert Sales Ops, review threshold decrease or lead generation quality"
    monitor: "CloudWatch dashboard: lead_qualification_recall metric"
    threshold: "Alert if recall < 0.60 for 7 consecutive days"
    severity: "medium"
    mitigation: "Monthly pipeline review with marketing team"
    
  - condition: "Market conditions change significantly (recession, industry shift)"
    impact: "high"
    probability: "low"
    action: "Re-run threshold validation experiment with recent data"
    monitor: "Quarterly business review, sales conversion trends"
    threshold: "If sales conversion rate drops below 12% (from 15%), re-validate"
    severity: "high"
    mitigation: "Quarterly threshold review process established"
    
  - condition: "Function latency exceeds 50ms (currently ~5ms)"
    impact: "low"
    probability: "very_low"
    action: "Profile function, identify bottleneck, optimize"
    monitor: "APM latency metrics (DataDog)"
    threshold: "Alert if p95 > 50ms"
    severity: "low"
    mitigation: "Function is extremely simple, latency risk very low"

# WHERE is this implemented?
implementation:
  current: "agent/lead_qualifier/scoring.py"
  function: "qualify_lead"
  class: "LeadQualifier"  # Function is method of this class
  lines: "45-78"
  version: 3
  tests: "tests/test_lead_qualification.py"
  documentation: "docs/agents/lead_qualifier_DESIGN.md"
  api_docs: "Generated from docstring via Sphinx"

# HOW to verify this works?
verification:
  test_cases:
    - name: "qualified_lead_happy_path"
      description: "Lead meets both thresholds - should qualify"
      input:
        lead:
          id: "lead_001"
          score: 0.75
          data_quality: 0.85
        config:
          score_threshold: 0.70
          data_quality_threshold: 0.80
      expected_output:
        qualified: true
        score: 0.75
        data_quality: 0.85
        reason: "Lead meets both score (0.75 >= 0.70) and data quality (0.85 >= 0.80) thresholds"
      validates_constraint: "precision_minimum"
      test_file: "tests/test_qualification.py::test_qualified_lead"
      
    - name: "low_score_disqualified"
      description: "Lead has low score but good data quality - should NOT qualify"
      input:
        lead:
          id: "lead_002"
          score: 0.65
          data_quality: 0.90
        config:
          score_threshold: 0.70
          data_quality_threshold: 0.80
      expected_output:
        qualified: false
        score: 0.65
        data_quality: 0.90
        reason: "Lead score (0.65) below threshold (0.70)"
      validates_constraint: "recall_minimum"
      test_file: "tests/test_qualification.py::test_low_score"
      
    - name: "low_data_quality_disqualified"
      description: "Lead has high score but poor data quality - should NOT qualify"
      input:
        lead:
          id: "lead_003"
          score: 0.85
          data_quality: 0.70
        config:
          score_threshold: 0.70
          data_quality_threshold: 0.80
      expected_output:
        qualified: false
        score: 0.85
        data_quality: 0.70
        reason: "Lead data quality (0.70) below threshold (0.80)"
      validates_constraint: "precision_minimum"
      test_file: "tests/test_qualification.py::test_low_data_quality"
      rationale: "This is incident #456 scenario - prevents frustration from missing contact info"
      
    - name: "boundary_case_exact_thresholds"
      description: "Lead exactly at thresholds - should qualify"
      input:
        lead:
          id: "lead_004"
          score: 0.70
          data_quality: 0.80
        config:
          score_threshold: 0.70
          data_quality_threshold: 0.80
      expected_output:
        qualified: true
        score: 0.70
        data_quality: 0.80
        reason: "Lead meets both score (0.70 >= 0.70) and data quality (0.80 >= 0.80) thresholds"
      validates_constraint: "determinism"
      test_file: "tests/test_qualification.py::test_boundary_exact"
      
    - name: "invalid_score_validation_error"
      description: "Lead with out-of-range score - should raise ValidationError"
      input:
        lead:
          id: "lead_005"
          score: 1.5  # Inva

re: 0.70
          data_quality: 0.80
        config:
          score_threshold: 0.70
          data_quality_threshold: 0.80
      expected_output:
        qualified: true
        score: 0.70
        data_quality: 0.80
        reason: "Lead meets both score (0.70 >= 0.70) and data quality (0.80 >= 0.80) thresholds"
      validates_constraint: "determinism"
      test_file: "tests/test_qualification.py::test_boundary_exact"
      
    - name: "invalid_score_validation_error"
      description: "Lead with out-of-range score - should raise ValidationError"
      input:
        lead:
          id: "lead_005"
          score: 1.5  # Invalid: > 1.0
          data_quality: 0.85
      expected_exception:
        type: "ValidationError"
        message: "lead.score must be between 0.0 and 1.0"
      validates_constraint: "type_safety"
      test_file: "tests/test_qualification.py::test_invalid_score"
      
    - name: "wrong_type_type_error"
      description: "Passing dict instead of Lead object - should raise TypeError"
      input:
        lead: {"id": "lead_006", "score": 0.75}  # Wrong type
      expected_exception:
        type: "TypeError"
        message: "Expected Lead, got dict"
      validates_constraint: "type_safety"
      test_file: "tests/test_qualification.py::test_wrong_type"
  
  property_tests:
    - property: "idempotency"
      description: "Calling function multiple times with same input produces same output"
      test: "tests/test_properties.py::test_qualify_lead_idempotent"
      validates_constraint: "determinism"
      
    - property: "commutativity_of_inputs"
      description: "Order of validation (score first vs data_quality first) doesn't matter"
      test: "tests/test_properties.py::test_validation_order_independent"
      validates_constraint: "determinism"
      
    - property: "monotonicity"
      description: "Higher score/data_quality never makes qualified become not qualified"
      test: "tests/test_properties.py::test_monotonic_thresholds"
      validates_constraint: "precision_minimum"
  
  integration_tests:
    - name: "end_to_end_qualification_flow"
      description: "Test full flow: Lead creation → Scoring → Qualification → Result"
      test: "tests/integration/test_lead_pipeline.py::test_qualification_flow"
      validates: "Integration with lead scoring system"
      
  performance_tests:
    - name: "latency_benchmark"
      description: "Measure p50, p95, p99 latency on 10,000 calls"
      test: "tests/test_performance.py::test_qualify_lead_latency"
      validates_constraint: "performance (latency < 10ms p95)"
      current_results: "p50: 3ms, p95: 7ms, p99: 15ms (PASS)"

    change: "Attempted ML-based classification with Random Forest"

# WHEN was this changed?
changelog:
  - version: 3
    date: "2025-12-15"
    change: "Implemented threshold-based qualification with data quality gate"
    reason: "ML classifier (v2) overfitted, rule-based (v1) too brittle"
    evidence: "playground/approach_comparison_2025-12.ipynb"
    approved_by: "VP Sales (Jane Smith)"
    migration_notes: "Deprecated v2 ML classifier, removed v1 rule engine"
    
  - version: 2
    date: "2025-12-01"
    change: "Attempted ML-based classification with Random Forest"
    reason: "Wanted adaptive qualification that improves over time"
    deprecated: "2025-12-15"
    deprecated_reason: "Overfitting issues, 78% production accuracy vs 92% training"
    lessons_learned: "ML requires larger dataset and more sophisticated validation"

    date: "2025-10-01"

    deprecated: "2025-11-15"

    lessons_learned: "Simple thresholds > complex rules for this use case"
